evals.prompt: Add autocomplete search to this site using Algolia.
evals.expected_output: Discovery questions followed by a full-featured autocomplete implementation using EXPERIMENTAL_Autocomplete from react-instantsearch
expectations: Asks for Algolia credentials (app ID, API key, index name) before writing any code
Asks whether a query suggestions index exists
Inspects the project's styling approach, breakpoints, and framework before building
Uses EXPERIMENTAL_Autocomplete from react-instantsearch, not @algolia/autocomplete-js
Uses InstantSearchNext from react-instantsearch-nextjs if the project uses Next.js App Router
Creates the search client with algoliasearch v5 outside of React components
Implements multiple sources (query suggestions + products) when a QS index is available
Implements item navigation with both getURL (keyboard Enter) and clickable links in itemComponent
Sets detachedMediaQuery for mobile full-viewport overlay
Applies mobile detached CSS (body lock, fixed container, flex-grow panel, absolute inner layout)

evals.prompt: I have a Next.js App Router site with Tailwind v4. Add an autocomplete search bar in the header that searches my products index and my query suggestions index. Products should show an image, highlighted name, and price. Clicking a product should go to /shop/[id].
evals.expected_output: Autocomplete with two sources, rich product previews, working navigation, correct Next.js and Tailwind v4 handling
expectations: Uses InstantSearchNext from react-instantsearch-nextjs, not InstantSearch
Places the InstantSearchNext provider high in the component tree (layout level), not wrapping only the autocomplete
Sets routing={true} on the provider
Sets insights={true} on the provider
Uses Highlight component for the product name, not raw text
Sets getURL on showQuerySuggestions to navigate to a search results page
Places ais-* styles outside of any @layer in the global CSS file (Tailwind v4 purges @layer content for unknown classes)
Greps node_modules/react-instantsearch for actual ais-* class names before writing CSS instead of guessing

evals.prompt: The autocomplete you built doesn't look right on mobile. The panel is cut off and I can't scroll through results.
evals.expected_output: Diagnoses missing mobile detached CSS and applies the correct flex-grow + absolute panel pattern
expectations: Identifies that the detached overlay needs explicit CSS for full-viewport layout
Adds body.ais-Autocomplete--detached styles to lock scroll
Uses flex-direction: column on the detached container with the panel set to flex-grow: 1
Uses position: absolute with inset 0 and overflow-y: auto on the inner panel layout
Does NOT set position: fixed on the panel itself (that breaks the flex layout)
Verifies the fix at a mobile breakpoint

evals.prompt: I want to add search to my React app. I have an Algolia index called 'movies' with title, overview, poster_path, release_date, and vote_average fields. No query suggestions index.
evals.expected_output: Discovery followed by autocomplete with single source, adapted to movie schema, no fabricated QS source
expectations: Does NOT fabricate a query suggestions source when told there is none
Adapts the result preview to movie attributes (poster image, highlighted title, release date, rating)
Asks where the autocomplete should be placed in the UI
Asks what the movie detail page route is (or what attribute to use for the URL slug)
Uses Highlight on the title attribute
Provides a no-results component

evals.prompt: Style the autocomplete to match my site's design. I have a dark theme with CSS custom properties for colors and spacing.
evals.expected_output: Styling via ais-* CSS selectors in the global stylesheet, using the project's design tokens
expectations: Writes CSS targeting ais-* selectors in the global stylesheet
Greps node_modules/react-instantsearch for actual ais-* class names before writing CSS
Uses the project's CSS custom properties for colors and spacing
Matches the dark theme
Does not guess ais-* class names
